children.finalMessage: {"verdict":"needs-attention","summary":"Security review found deployable secrets, unauthenticated live-trading triggers, weak secret encryption, and insufficient validation at trading trust boundaries.","findings":[{"file":"wrangler.toml","line_start":12,"line_end":13,"severity":"critical","title":"Deployable JWT and encryption secrets are committed in Worker vars","description":"The Worker config contains concrete JWT_SECRET and ENCRYPTION_KEY values under [vars]. Wrangler vars are part of deployed configuration unless explicitly overridden, so a deployment using these defaults would let anyone with source access forge session JWTs and decrypt stored exchange/API signer credentials if database data is exposed.","recommendation":"Remove secrets from wrangler.toml entirely. Require JWT_SECRET and ENCRYPTION_KEY as Cloudflare Worker secrets, fail startup when they are missing or known development defaults, and rotate any production credentials if this file was ever deployed."},{"file":"src/server/worker.ts","line_start":26,"line_end":30,"severity":"high","title":"Unauthenticated HTTP endpoint can trigger scraper-driven live execution","description":"POST /api/scraper/run is public and calls runCoinlegsScraper(), which can create TradeIntents and dispatch execution jobs to active exchange connections. Any external caller can force the live trading pipeline to run outside the scheduled control path.","recommendation":"Remove this endpoint from production or protect it with an admin procedure/cron-only shared secret. Add rate limiting and audit any manual execution trigger separately from scheduled execution."},{"file":"src/server/coinlegs-scraper.ts","line_start":25,"line_end":105,"severity":"high","title":"Unsigned external signal feed is trusted to create live trade intents","description":"The scraper fetches JSON from an external Worker and directly maps qualifying records into TradeIntents, including the trading symbol, then dispatches them to connected exchange accounts. There is no payload signature, schema validation, replay guard beyond signalId insertion, or symbol allowlist before orders are created.","recommendation":"Treat the signal feed as untrusted input: verify signed payloads, validate with a strict schema, enforce exchange-specific symbol allowlists and side/order constraints, reject stale/replayed signals, and separate ingestion from live dispatch with an explicit approval or hardened policy gate."},{"file":"src/server/db.ts","line_start":34,"line_end":65,"severity":"high","title":"Stored API keys use unauthenticated AES-CBC with weak key derivation and fallback","description":"Exchange API secrets and signer private keys are encrypted with AES-CBC without authentication, using a key made by truncating/padding ENCRYPTION_KEY and falling back to JWT_SECRET. This weakens at-rest protection and allows ciphertext tampering to go undetected.","recommendation":"Use AES-GCM or another AEAD mode with random nonces and authenticated metadata. Derive keys with HKDF/PBKDF where appropriate, never fall back to JWT_SECRET, validate key length/entropy at startup, and plan a migration/rotation for existing ciphertexts."},{"file":"src/server/cex/signing.ts","line_start":69,"line_end":76,"severity":"medium","title":"Signed exchange query strings are built without URL encoding","description":"Binance signed query parameters are interpolated as raw key=value pairs. Because symbols and other order fields can originate from admin input or the external signal feed, reserved characters could alter the signed query structure or produce exchange-side parameter confusion.","recommendation":"Construct all exchange request query strings with URLSearchParams or strict percent-encoding before signing, and validate all order fields against exchange-specific regexes before they reach the signing layer."},{"file":"src/server/_core/cookies.ts","line_start":1,"line_end":10,"severity":"medium","title":"Cookie sessions allow cross-site use without CSRF protection","description":"Secure sessions are issued with SameSite=None, while state-changing tRPC mutations rely on cookie authentication and no CSRF token check was found. This increases exposure if CORS/origin assumptions change or a browser sends credentialed cross-site requests.","recommendation":"Prefer SameSite=Lax or Strict for the session cookie unless cross-site embedding is required. Add CSRF tokens or origin checks for all cookie-authenticated mutations, especially trading, credential, and account-management actions."}],"next_steps":["Remove committed secrets and rotate deployed JWT/encryption keys.","Disable or authenticate /api/scraper/run before any production deployment.","Add signed-feed verification, strict schema validation, and symbol allowlists before live dispatch.","Migrate encrypted credentials to AEAD encryption with a dedicated required secret.","Add CSRF protection and exchange request encoding tests."]}
